pattern: Slide across the array while tracking the smallest and largest value seen far enough to the left, so each new position only ever needs to compare itself against those two running extremes instead of every earlier position.
hints: As you scan forward, only positions that are already at least indexDifference behind the current one are allowed to pair with it, so keep a window of eligible earlier positions.

Within that eligible window you never need to remember every value -- only the smallest and the largest, since any valid pairing with the current position will show up against one of those two extremes.

At each new position, update the running smallest/largest eligible index first, then check whether the current value is far enough from either extreme; return the first pair that qualifies, or [-1, -1] if the scan finishes with none.

edge_inputs.description: a typical case where the required index gap and value gap are both satisfied by one pair
a zero index gap and zero value gap let an index pair with itself
no pair anywhere in the array can satisfy both required gaps at once
the required index gap exceeds what any pair of positions in the array can reach, regardless of how small the value gap is
with no value gap required, any sufficiently separated pair works even when every value is identical
values sitting at the extreme ends of the allowed range still satisfy the maximum required value gap
several different index pairs are all equally valid answers here
at the maximum array length, only the two indices at the very ends of the array are spaced far enough apart to qualify

stress.description: random arrays with random index and value difference requirements, up to the maximum array length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    indexDifference = rng.randint(0, 100)
    valueDifference = rng.randint(0, 50)
    return [nums, indexDifference, valueDifference]
